title: THIS Is Why Civilization May Have Started Much EARLIER Than We Think
summary: This short clip discusses the evolving understanding of when human civilization began, arguing that archaeological discoveries continue to push back the timeline. The speaker uses Göbekli Tepe as evidence that complex construction existed far earlier than the traditional 6,000-year timeline suggests.

events.description: Göbekli Tepe was excavated in the 90s

key_claims.claim: The concept that civilization started in the Fertile Crescent 6,000 years ago was only established 200 years ago with the discovery of Assyrian and Babylonian capital cities
Göbekli Tepe is an ancient burial site in modern Turkey dating as far back as 9000 BC involving complex assembly of 50 ton blocks
Archaeological discoveries will likely find architecture predating Göbekli Tepe within the next hundred years
